methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: X-Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (IPO), post-money $2B+

data.narrative:
Deep Fission, a nuclear energy company at IPO stage founded by Liz Muller and Rich Muller, presents a valuation challenge given the nascent state of its comparable universe. The broader nuclear energy sector includes 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies, but the peer-median approach is inapplicable here. The private peer set, which includes X-Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy, lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only 1 company meets the narrow stage filter, falling well short of the 3-company minimum needed for reliable median triangulation. Expanding to all-sector peers would introduce unacceptable methodological drift and obscure sector-specific dynamics.

Given Deep Fission's $80M in cumulative fundraising and the absence of robust peer multiples, valuation must rely on alternative approaches not detailed in this dataset. The primary uncertainty is the thinness of the nuclear energy IPO pipeline; only three public comparables exist, and their trading multiples may not yet reflect Deep Fission's specific technology maturity, regulatory pathway, or capital requirements. Any valuation range derived here would require supplemental methods—discounted cash flow analysis, precedent transactions within the private nuclear cohort, or constructive bottom-up modeling—to establish defensible support. The market dislocation between private and public nuclear companies remains substantial, creating estimation risk at IPO pricing.
